name: Struggling to find a job? Try looking in Nevada
summary: A sparsely-populated state known for its world-class casinos and dry desert climate has been a bright spot in the tepid U.S. job market.

name: Hidden stress burden: 1 in 5 South Asians in America face health challenges, new study finds
summary: Among all the stressors identified, intergenerational conflict emerged as one of the most significant.

name: Eye on Vision: Regenerating Eye Tissue In Frogs And Delay In Web Accessibility Requirements Taking Effect
summary: Dr. Kelly Tseng a professor in the School Of Life Sciences at the University of Nevada-Las Vegas, discusses how discovering the method in which frogs are able to regenerate eye tissue could lead to finding out how to replicate the process in humans.

name: FDA Approves Drug for Alzheimer's Agitation
summary: The extended-release oral combination of dextromethorphan-bupropion uses a different mechanism of action than antipsychotic medications, which clinicians sometimes prescribe for patients with Alzheimer's disease who also experience agitation. But whether the new medication will be used as a first-line treatment is up for debate.

name: UNLV outlines plan for downtown Las Vegas hub at Civic Plaza campus
summary: UNLV leaders presented an update Wednesday to the Las Vegas City Council on UNLV @ Civic Plaza, a proposed downtown satellite campus at the city’s Civic Center and Plaza.

name: Anti-Uranium Summit Sheds Light on Toxic Industry
summary: Energy Fuels mines uranium-laden limestone found in the area around Red Butte in a process known as breccia pipe mining. David Kramer, a hydrogeologist at the University of Nevada Las Vegas, explained the process to a crowd of attendees at the summit.

name: Elon Musk's Vegas Loop: A Neoliberal Nightmare or Futuristic Transport?
summary: A ride on Elon Musk's Vegas Loop reveals an underwhelming, slow tunnel system far from original promises. Critics call it a private transit scam benefiting developers, not the public.

name: Attorney breaks down responsible artificial intelligence practices in legal guidance
summary: Law school students in Las Vegas will soon be schooled on AI. Beginning in the fall, the William S. Boyd School of Law at UNLV will require a new course on the rise of artificial intelligence. School officials say it's designed to prepare law students for one of the biggest shifts currently reshaping the legal profession.

name: Fury as consumers learn Uber and Lyft charge different prices for the same rides and offer deceptive discounts
summary: An analysis by researchers at the University of Nevada, Las Vegas, found that 12 percent of Uber rides and 21 percent of Lyft rides displayed discounts - a significant increase from levels recorded just two years earlier.
